stories.title: SOCSO Foreign Worker Invalidity Scheme Inclusion Flag and Rate Update (July 2024)
stories.description: Effective July 1, 2024, PERKESO extended the SOCSO Invalidity Scheme to foreign workers under age 60, who were previously only covered under the Employment Injury Scheme alone. Employers must now contribute at the full First Category rate (Employment Injury + Invalidity combined) for eligible foreign workers, and the PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman upload must use the correct scheme category code per employee. Any employer still remitting at the old employment-injury-only rate is under-contributing and faces PERKESO penalty exposure from the enforcement date.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType includes a socso_scheme_category Select field ('First Category (EI + Invalidity)' / 'Second Category (EI Only)'); foreign workers under age 60 hired on or after 2024-07-01 default to 'First Category'
PCB/payroll engine computes SOCSO contribution using the correct First Category rate for eligible foreign workers (combined EI + Invalidity scheme contribution amount from Jadual Kadar Caruman)
PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman TXT upload uses correct scheme code per employee based on socso_scheme_category and age at payroll run date
Existing foreign employees hired before July 2024 with blank or incorrect socso_scheme_category are flagged in a migration report for HR review
A hard validation warning fires on Salary Slip submission if a foreign worker under age 60 has socso_scheme_category set to 'Second Category'
technicalNotes: PERKESO official FAQ for foreign worker Invalidity Scheme extension: https://www.perkeso.gov.my/images/pekerja_asing/250724%20-FAQ%20-%20INVALIDITY%20SCHEME%20FOREIGN%20WORKER.pdf
First Category (under 60): employer 1.75%, employee 0.5% of insured wages — verify actual jadual amounts from https://www.perkeso.gov.my/en/rate-of-contribution.html
Second Category (60 and above): employer 1.25%, employee 0% — employment injury only, unchanged
Vialto Partners alert on the extension: https://vialtopartners.com/regional-alerts/malaysia-employment-tax-extension-of-invalidity-scheme-coverage-to-foreign-workers
Custom field: custom_socso_scheme_category on Employee DocType; trigger auto-assignment from nationality + date_of_birth + date_of_joining
dependencies: Generate SOCSO and EIS e-Caruman Upload File (PERKESO ASSIST Portal)

stories.title: MTD Method 3 — PCB Computation for Weekly and Fortnightly Payroll Periods
stories.description: The LHDN PCB specification defines three calculation methods: Method 1 (standard monthly), Method 2 (YTD recalculation), and Method 3 for employees paid on a non-monthly basis such as weekly or fortnightly cycles. Method 3 annualizes the periodic gross pay, computes annual MTD using the Schedule 1 formula, then converts back to the pay-period amount. Without this method, employers running weekly or fortnightly payroll have no compliant mechanism to determine the correct PCB per pay run, defaulting incorrectly to a monthly annualization that over- or under-deducts.
acceptanceCriteria: Payroll Entry and Payroll Period support pay_period_type (Monthly / Weekly / Fortnightly / Other) and pay_period_days fields

When pay_period_type is not Monthly, PCB engine applies Method 3: annualized_gross = periodic_gross × (365 / pay_period_days); annual MTD computed from annualized amount less reliefs; period MTD = annual_MTD × (pay_period_days / 365)
CP39 submission correctly aggregates multiple weekly/fortnightly runs within the same calendar month into a single monthly CP39 row per employee
EA Form correctly sums all pay-period MTDs for the assessment year totals regardless of pay frequency
Unit test: employee earning RM1,000 per week produces the same annual MTD as an equivalent monthly employee at RM4,333/month within a RM10 rounding tolerance
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB 2025 Specification PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
Method 3 is defined in the LHDN computerised MTD specification under 'Kaedah Pengiraan Untuk Tempoh Gaji Bukan Bulanan'
Monthly annualization: multiply weekly pay × 52 or fortnightly × 26; then divide annual MTD back by 52 or 26 for per-period deduction
CP39 month mapping: all weekly pay runs in e.g., March → single CP39 March row; aggregate MTD and wages
Custom field on Payroll Period doctype: pay_period_type (Select); default 'Monthly' for backward compatibility

stories.title: EPF i-Akaun Contribution File Whole Ringgit Rounding Rule Enforcement
stories.description: KWSP mandates that all EPF contribution amounts must be paid and submitted in whole Ringgit Malaysia — no sen (cents) are accepted in the i-Akaun TXT/CSV upload file or during payment. The i-Akaun file generator must apply the correct rounding to both employer and employee contribution values before writing them to the file. Currently the system may generate decimal values causing file validation rejection by the KWSP portal, requiring HR to manually correct and re-upload the file each month.
acceptanceCriteria: EPF i-Akaun CSV/TXT file generator rounds each employee's employer-share and employee-share EPF amounts independently to the nearest whole Ringgit (standard arithmetic: ≥ 0.50 rounds up, < 0.50 truncates)
Voluntary excess contributions (i-Topup) are also rounded to whole Ringgit before file output
A per-payroll-run EPF Rounding Summary report shows sen absorbed or lost per employee line, with a batch total, for auditing
File upload to KWSP i-Akaun succeeds without format validation rejection due to decimal values in any contribution column
Unit test: employee salary RM3,333.33 → employee EPF at 11% = RM366.667 → file output RM367; employer at 13% = RM433.333 → file output RM433
technicalNotes: KWSP official rule: 'Contributions must be paid in Ringgit Malaysia (RM) only, without cents, before the 15th of the following month'
Source: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer/responsibilities/mandatory-contribution
Apply rounding at file-generation time only — payroll internal amounts must retain full precision (sens) for payslip accuracy and EPF balance reconciliation
The rounding sen difference is a legitimate cost/credit variance — it is NOT deducted additionally from the employee
Both the TXT (legacy) and CSV (current) i-Akaun formats require whole ringgit contribution columns
dependencies: Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File

stories.title: Progressive Wage Policy Government Incentive Subsidy Claim Tracking Workflow
stories.description: The Progressive Wage Policy (PWP), expanded in 2025 with RM200 million budget, provides participating employers a government incentive of RM300 per eligible employee per month for up to 12 months. Eligible employees must earn between RM1,500 and RM4,999/month and have at least 12 months' service. Participating companies must provide skill-development training as a condition. The payroll system currently tracks only the PWP eligibility flag but has no workflow for subsidy claim application submission, per-employee entitlement tracking, or alert when the 12-month subsidy claim window nears expiry.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType includes custom_pwp_enrolled (boolean), custom_pwp_enrollment_date, custom_pwp_approval_reference (from MOHR portal), and custom_pwp_subsidy_months_claimed (0–12)
PWP Eligibility Report auto-identifies employees earning RM1,500–RM4,999 with ≥12 months service and flags those not yet enrolled for HR action
Monthly PWP Claim Schedule report generates a summary: enrolled employee count, RM300 × months per employee, cumulative total subsidy entitlement vs claimed to date
An alert fires when a PWP-enrolled employee's salary revision takes them above RM4,999 (exits the subsidy band) or when their 12-month claim window is within 2 months of expiry
Training Evidence checkbox on Employee allows HR to mark that the required skill training evidence has been recorded as a condition of PWP participation
technicalNotes: PWP is administered by MOHR / TalentCorp — employers apply via the MOHR portal and receive an approval reference
Latest expansion: RM200 million budget targeting 50,000 workers, salary increase from RM1,900 to RM2,200 at entry level confirmed as of Dec 2025
Source: Malay Mail report (23 Feb 2026): https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/02/23/pay-rises-on-the-way-progressive-wage-policy-to-boost-salaries-for-over-50000-workers-by-2027/210097
This story extends 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report' with the claim-tracking operational workflow
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report

stories.title: MyInvois SDK v2.1 Compliance Validation for Payroll Self-Billed Invoice Submissions
stories.description: On 6 April 2025, IRBM published updated MyInvois 2.1 guidelines with additional mandatory field requirements and clarifications on B2B transaction types including self-billed invoices — the mechanism used for payroll-related expense reimbursements. The payroll integration's XML builder and API client must be validated against the v2.1 specification to ensure continued acceptance by MyInvois without rejection. Any new mandatory fields, changed InvoiceTypeCode values, or updated schemeID vocabulary must be reflected in the builder.
acceptanceCriteria: All self-billed XML documents generated by the lhdn_payroll_integration pass LHDN MyInvois v2.1 schema validation with zero errors
Any new mandatory fields introduced in v2.1 (e.g., additional party identification, classification codes for payroll self-billing) are populated in the XML builder
API client uses the correct SDK v2.1 version-specific endpoint URLs and request headers as per the updated SDK documentation
Existing integration tests (test_intg_scenarios.py T-01 through T-05) continue to pass against v2.1 mock and sandbox responses without modification
The LHDN configuration DocType stores an sdk_version field set to '2.1' to make the active specification version auditable
technicalNotes: IRBM issued updated guidelines on 6 April 2025 — EY Malaysia tax alert: https://www.ey.com/en_my/technical/tax-alerts/the-inland-revenue-board-s-updated-guidelines-on-e-invoices-and-the-software-development-kit-july
Official MyInvois SDK documentation: https://sdk.myinvois.hasil.gov.my
Key areas to verify against v2.1: InvoiceTypeCode options for self-billing, PartyIdentification schemeID vocabulary, mandatory vs optional field changes, pagination/error response format changes
Self-billed invoices in payroll context: salary slips (self-billed by employer for employee remuneration) and expense claim reimbursements
dependencies: Fix token expiry check and status poller base_url bug
Assess and Implement XAdES XML Digital Signature for MyInvois Self-Billed Phase 2

stories.title: Retroactive Salary Arrears PCB Back-Period Spread Calculation
stories.description: When employers award retroactive salary increases (back pay covering prior months within the same tax year), LHDN guidance allows the additional income to be spread across the months it was earned for MTD recalculation, avoiding a PCB spike in the month of payment. Currently the system treats all retroactive pay as additional income in the month paid (using the bonus/irregular pay pathway), which can cause significant over-deduction in a single month and employee hardship. A retroactive pay workflow that re-computes MTD for each affected prior month and generates corrected CP39 amendment rows is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Slip includes custom_retroactive_amount, custom_retroactive_period_from, and custom_retroactive_period_to fields
When retroactive_amount > 0, system offers two modes: (a) lump-sum in current month (existing behaviour), or (b) back-period spread — the user selects

In back-period spread mode, system recomputes MTD for each affected prior month by adding proportional retroactive pay to that month's gross, recalculates tax, and collects the incremental MTD as retroactive_pcb_recovery in the current month's Salary Slip
CP39 file includes corrected prior-month rows (one per retroactive month) via the LHDN e-PCB Plus amendment submission format
EA Form classifies retroactive pay under the assessment year it was earned (not year paid) when both fall within the same calendar year
technicalNotes: LHDN guidance: retroactive pay covering prior months in same year may be spread across those months for MTD recalculation — reduces current-month PCB spike
Reference: Talenox LHDN PCB guide: https://blog.talenox.com/malaysia-tax-guide-how-do-i-calculate-pcb-mtd-part-2-of-3/
Cross-year retroactive pay (e.g., 2025 arrears paid in 2026) is taxed in year received — this edge case must be documented and handled separately
LHDN e-PCB Plus allows prior month amendment submissions — use this pathway for retroactive month CP39 corrections
This story complements 'e-PCB Plus Prior Month PCB Amendment' by providing the payroll-side calculation that drives what needs to be amended
dependencies: Generate CP39 monthly PCB remittance file
e-PCB Plus Prior Month PCB Amendment — Trigger Corrected CP39 Row on Salary Slip Resubmission

stories.title: Foreign Worker EPF 2% Three-Account Allocation Split for i-Akaun CSV Export
stories.description: From October 1, 2025, non-Malaysian employees with eligible employment passes must contribute 2% EPF (employer 2%, employee 2%). KWSP applies the same three-account structure (Akaun Persaraan / Akaun Sejahtera / Akaun Fleksibel) to these foreign worker contributions, but the allocation proportions for the 2% rate may differ from the Malaysian citizen standard (75%/15%/10%). The existing EPF i-Akaun CSV story handles the Malaysian three-account split — this story ensures the file correctly encodes foreign worker 2% contributions in the right account columns without contaminating the Malaysian employee rows.
acceptanceCriteria: For employees with nationality != Malaysian and eligible pass type (EP, VP(TE), etc.) with hire/contribution date >= 2025-10-01, EPF contribution rate is 2% each for employer and employee
The i-Akaun CSV rows for foreign workers use the KWSP-specified account allocation columns for the 2% rate — verified from the latest KWSP circular on foreign worker three-account allocation
Payslip shows three-account EPF breakdown for foreign workers: Akaun Persaraan, Akaun Sejahtera, Akaun Fleksibel at the correct proportions of the 2% deduction
A Salary Slip validation warning fires if a non-domestic-servant foreign worker with a valid employment pass shows EPF contribution = 0 for any payroll period from October 2025 onward
Unit test: foreign worker at RM4,000/month → employee EPF RM80 (2%), employer EPF RM80 (2%); CSV row account allocation sums to RM80 employee / RM80 employer exactly
technicalNotes: KWSP mandatory EPF for foreign employees announcement: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer/responsibilities/mandatory-contribution
Domestic servants (maids, cooks, cleaners, gardeners) are EXCLUDED — verify custom_is_domestic_servant flag on Employee
KWSP circular or FAQ needed to confirm three-account split proportions for 2% rate — may match 75/15/10 or differ; check against latest KWSP communications
This story extends 'Update EPF i-Akaun Employer Contribution CSV Upload Format for Three-Account (75/15/10) Allocation' specifically for foreign worker 2% rate rows in the same file
The EPF Akaun Fleksibel enables early withdrawal — foreign workers may not be eligible for the same withdrawal rules; noted as informational only
dependencies: Implement Foreign Worker EPF Mandatory Contribution (Effective October 2025)
Update EPF i-Akaun Employer Contribution CSV Upload Format for Three-Account (75/15/10) Allocation
Foreign Worker EPF Oct 2025 Mandate: Domestic Servant Category Exclusion Flag

stories.title: e-Invoice Mandatory Phase Readiness Check for Employer Company Records
stories.description: LHDN's e-invoice mandate rolls out in phases based on annual turnover: Phase 1 (>RM100M, Aug 2024), Phase 2 (>RM25M, Jan 2025), Phase 3 (>RM150K–RM500K, July 2025), and Phase 4+ (all remaining businesses). Many employers using the payroll app are SMEs who crossed Phase 3 threshold in July 2025 and must now have MyInvois credentials configured. The payroll system should detect whether the employer's revenue tier requires active e-invoice compliance and warn HR if MyInvois API credentials are missing or the configuration is incomplete.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType includes custom_annual_revenue (Currency) and a read-only custom_einvoice_mandate_phase (Data) field auto-derived from revenue: '>RM100M (Phase 1)', '>RM25M (Phase 2)', '>RM150K (Phase 3)', 'All Businesses (Phase 4+)', or 'Below RM150K (Voluntary)'

On Company save, if einvoice_mandate_phase is not 'Below RM150K' and custom_client_id or custom_client_secret is blank, a prominent warning banner is shown: 'e-Invoice mandatory for this company — MyInvois API credentials are missing'
An e-Invoice Readiness Report lists all Companies in the system, their mandate phase, and compliance status (Compliant / Credentials Missing / Voluntary / Not Applicable)
Alert email is sent to HR Admin when a Company's custom_annual_revenue change moves it into a new mandatory phase
The readiness check is a pre-flight guard only — it does not prevent payroll runs but flags the MyInvois gap for action
technicalNotes: LHDN e-Invoice phase schedule: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/e-invoice/
Phase 3 (from July 2025): taxpayers with annual revenue RM150K–RM500K are now mandatory
Phase 4/5 (all remaining taxpayers with any turnover): LHDN has not finalized the date for the final phase — configure as 'TBD' or rely on LHDN announcement
This check complements the existing myinvois_erpgulf app setup — it validates the payroll employer configuration, not the e-invoice submission flow itself

stories.title: Mid-Year TP1 Relief Amendment PCB Recalculation and Adjustment Schedule
stories.description: When an employee submits an amended TP1 form mid-year (e.g., adding a new dependent, claiming a new relief category, or correcting a relief amount), the MTD calculation for all remaining months of the year must be recalculated to reflect the updated annual tax liability. Without a structured recalculation workflow, HR must manually adjust PCB in subsequent Salary Slips — a process prone to errors and omissions. This story implements a TP1 amendment detection, recalculation, and schedule-approval workflow that automates the MTD adjustment across remaining payroll months.
acceptanceCriteria: When a TP1 form's status changes to 'Amended' or a new TP1 is submitted mid-year, a 'PCB Recalculation Pending' flag is set on the employee's active payroll record

HR can trigger a 'Recalculate Remaining MTD' action that computes: revised annual MTD using updated TP1 relief totals, less MTD already deducted YTD, and spreads the remainder equally across remaining months
The system generates a PCB Adjustment Schedule (tabular: month, old MTD, new MTD, difference) that HR must review and approve before it applies to future Salary Slips
Upon approval, subsequent Salary Slips for that employee automatically use the revised monthly MTD without manual intervention
An audit log entry records: TP1 submission date, previous total reliefs, new total reliefs, MTD YTD at change date, revised monthly MTD, and approving HR user
technicalNotes: MTD recalculation formula: Revised_monthly_MTD = (Revised_annual_MTD - MTD_already_deducted_YTD) / remaining_payroll_months_in_year

Edge case: revised annual MTD < MTD already deducted (overpayment) → employee gets reduced or zero MTD for remaining months; refund via payroll is NOT standard — employee must claim via annual tax filing
TP1(1/2026) updates reference: https://support.briohr.com/knowledge/payroll-malaysia-key-tax-relief-updates-for-the-year-2026
LHDN employer payroll data specification: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/employer-payroll-data-specification/
This story is operationally distinct from 'Alert on Stale TP1 Relief Declarations' — that story alerts at year start; this story handles in-year amendments and drives MTD recalculation
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Alert on Stale TP1 Relief Declarations When New Assessment Year Begins Without Employee Re-Submission

stories.title: Value of Living Accommodation (VOLA) Prescribed Value Calculation Module for EA Form Part C
stories.description: LHDN Public Ruling PR 3/2005 (on Value of Living Accommodation) requires employer-provided housing to be declared as taxable employment income on EA Form Part C. The taxable VOLA is calculated using either: (a) Formula Method: Defined Rate × Annual Value of Accommodation, capped at 30% of the employee's gross annual employment income; or (b) actual rent paid by employer. Without this module, employers providing company housing or housing subsidies are unable to correctly populate Part C of the EA Form and underreport taxable BIK to LHDN. This is distinct from the general BIK module (which covers motor vehicles) and the VOLA election via TP2.
acceptanceCriteria: A VOLA configuration section on Employee or its annual payroll record captures: accommodation_type (Employer-Owned / Employer-Rented / Employee-Rented-with-Subsidy), annual_property_value, actual_annual_rent_paid_by_employer, employee_rent_contribution_annual, and days_occupied
For Employer-Owned: VOLA = min(Defined Rate × annual_value × days/365, 30% of annual gross income from employment)
For Employer-Rented: VOLA = actual annual rent paid by employer (no formula cap applies)
For Subsidized: VOLA = employer-paid portion only (actual rent minus employee contribution)
EA Form Part C (accommodation sub-section) is auto-populated with the computed VOLA amount from the above calculation
Unit test: employee gross RM60,000/year; employer-owned property annual value RM24,000; defined rate 30%: formula = 30% × RM24,000 = RM7,200; 30% cap = RM18,000; VOLA = RM7,200 (formula lower)
technicalNotes: LHDN Public Ruling PR 3/2005 'Value of Living Accommodation' — primary authority for VOLA calculation
LHDN BIK Public Ruling 3/2013 (covers VOLA and perquisites): https://phl.hasil.gov.my/pdf/pdfam/PR3_2013.pdf
Defined Rate per LHDN for accommodation: varies by accommodation type and annual value — verify from latest LHDN Public Ruling
VOLA is declared in EA Form Part C (separate from BIK motor vehicle which is Part C motor vehicle sub-section)
This module extends 'Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module' specifically for the living accommodation category
Talenox VOLA guide: https://help.talenox.com/en/articles/8290345-what-is-the-value-of-living-accommodation-vola-malaysia
dependencies: Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields
Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
